title: Falling
excerpt: Falling is forced downward movement caused by loss of support or stable flight, often ending in impact damage, collision, or prone state changes.
markdown:
# Falling

Falling is forced downward movement that begins when a creature or object loses support, loses stable flight, or is no longer held in a valid position. It is a [[hazards|hazard]] because the descent itself and the impact at the end can both change the world state abruptly.

## When Falling Starts

A fall usually begins when a surface gives way, a creature moves beyond secure footing, or aerial movement fails. A creature using [[flying|Flying]] can fall if it loses the state required to remain aloft, while a creature that can [[hover|Hover]] is harder to force downward just because it stops moving.

## Common Outcomes

- the falling creature is moved into lower space until something stops the descent
- impact at the end of the fall can deal damage or trigger other collision consequences
- the creature can end the fall [[prone_condition|Prone]] if the landing leaves it sprawled or destabilized

## Landing State

The final result depends on what occupies the destination, whether the landing space is [[occupied_space|occupied]], whether another effect interrupts the descent, and whether the creature reaches a surface, fluid, structure, or other obstacle before the fall would otherwise continue.
